subject: OBSERVATION: .watcher-seen.json causing unnecessary pre-sync commits on chromabook

body.summary: Follow-up to turn 1 (git-sync failures). Observed 11 autonomous pre-sync commits in ~2 hours on psychology-agent chromabook. Most changed only .watcher-seen.json, not MANIFEST.json. Root cause hypothesis: .watcher-seen.json updates on every cron tick, autonomous-sync.sh detects dirty tree, commits before pull, pushes. This creates commit noise without transport value.

evidence.pattern: cron tick -> watcher updates -> pre-sync detects dirty tree -> auto-commit -> push -> repeat

proposed_fixes: Add .watcher-seen.json to autonomous-sync.sh pre-commit exclusion list (git checkout -- .watcher-seen.json before dirty-tree check)
Or: gitignore .watcher-seen.json if it serves no cross-machine purpose
Or: move watcher state to /tmp/ (volatile, no git involvement)
body.relationship_to_turn_1: Turn 1 reported dirty-tree rebase failures. This observation identifies .watcher-seen.json as the likely source of the dirty tree — it changes every cron tick, creating the unstaged changes that block pull-with-rebase.

epistemic_flags: Root cause remains a hypothesis — .watcher-seen.json confirmed as most-changed file but other sources of dirty tree may exist
Fix choice (exclusion vs gitignore vs /tmp) depends on whether .watcher-seen.json serves a purpose beyond the local machine
